title: How do I access ArcGIS?
plain_text: ESRI's ArcGIS is a leading geographic information system (GIS) software with tools and capabilities for gathering, editing, analyzing, and modeling data. At no additional cost, members of the Northeastern community can access ArcGIS online and download ArcGIS Pro. For more information about ArcGIS and GIS in general, please visit Northeastern Library's GIS page. Table of Contents: Access ArcGIS account, Download ArcGIS Pro, Northeastern University Academic GIS Hub. Access ArcGIS account: Navigate to to ArcGIS Online. Sign in with your Northeastern credentials. Refer to with your Northeastern credentials. Refer to How do I log in to Northeastern services? for assistance. Download ArcGIS Pro: Note: ArcGIS Pro is configured to run on Windows and does not support installation on Mac. If you are a Mac user, you may need to run a parallel Windows on your system or sign in to your Northeastern University Virtual Desktop (Azure). Learn more about running ArcGIS Pro on a Mac and refer to How do I install Windows on a Mac? and How do I access Azure lab? for additional resources. Navigate to the to the Obtain and Download GIS page on the Northeastern Library website. Follow the instructions under the 'Download and Sign-in ArcGIS Pro' section. Note: If you encounter an error related to your ArcGIS account, contact the GIS specialist listed on the Northeastern Library's GIS Home page. For guided lessons and quick-start tutorials, visit the Northeastern Library's GIS Tutorials page. Northeastern University Academic GIS Hub: The Northeastern University GIS Hub serves as a centralized destination for discovering and downloading geographic data, as well as accessing interactive mapping applications and web map services.
body_html:
ESRI's ArcGIS is a leading geographic information system (GIS) software with tools and capabilities for gathering, editing, analyzing, and modeling data. At no additional cost, members of the Northeastern community can access ArcGIS online and download ArcGIS Pro. For more information about ArcGIS and GIS in general, please visit Northeastern Library's GIS page.

Table of Contents

  • Access ArcGIS account
  • Download ArcGIS Pro
  • Northeastern University Academic GIS Hub

Access ArcGIS account

  • Navigate to to ArcGIS Online.
  • Sign in with your Northeastern credentials. Refer to with your Northeastern credentials. Refer to How do I log in to Northeastern services? for assistance.

Download ArcGIS Pro

Note: ArcGIS Pro is configured to run on Windows and does not support installation on Mac. If you are a Mac user, you may need to run a parallel Windows on your system or sign in to your Northeastern University Virtual Desktop (Azure). Learn more about running ArcGIS Pro on a Mac and refer to How do I install Windows on a Mac? and How do I access Azure lab? for additional resources.
  • Navigate to the to the Obtain and Download GIS page on the Northeastern Library website.
  • Follow the instructions under the 'Download and Sign-in ArcGIS Pro' section.
Note: If you encounter an error related to your ArcGIS account, contact the GIS specialist listed on the Northeastern Library's GIS Home page. For guided lessons and quick-start tutorials, visit the Northeastern Library's GIS Tutorials page.

Northeastern University Academic GIS Hub

The Northeastern University GIS Hub serves as a centralized destination for discovering and downloading geographic data, as well as accessing interactive mapping applications and web map services.
